expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: analysis.md (65 lines) and architecture.md (223 lines) both contain substantive content
Selected: Microservices Architecture. Justified by scalability demands, team autonomy, K8s experience, and Strangler Fig migration.

expectations.text: A2: Ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Only 4 styles evaluated: Modular Monolith, Service-Based, Microservices, Event-Driven Microservices. Missing: Layered, Microkernel, Service-Oriented, Space-Based.

expectations.text: A3: Fitness function table with style-specific structural FFs (not just characteristic FFs)
expectations.evidence: 6 structural FFs: Service Coupling (no cross-service internal imports, max 2 sync deps), Independent Deployability (no cross-service DB FKs, startup isolation), Data Isolation (unique DB credentials, K8s NetworkPolicies blocking cross-service DB access), API Contract Stability (consumer-driven contracts, breaking change detection), Service Size Guard (LOC/endpoint/table limits). All are structural invariants.

expectations.text: A4: Style fitness functions are specific to the selected style (microservices)
expectations.evidence: FFs enforce microservices-specific invariants: per-service data isolation with network policies, independent deployability with circuit breaker startup test, service size guards to prevent mini-monoliths, consumer-driven contract testing between services.

expectations.text: A5: Fitness functions include Tool/Approach or implementation guidance
expectations.evidence: Detailed YAML implementation blocks for each FF with specific tools: archunit, Pact, Flyway/Liquibase migration scanner, K8s NetworkPolicies validation, cloc, Gatling. Far exceeds minimum requirement.

expectations.text: A6: N/A - not an evolution path scenario
expectations.evidence: Eval 1 is not an evolution path scenario (evals 4 and 5 only)
